reasoning: The Dow Jones Industrial Average historically has a positive drift, which makes the probability of an increase greater than 0.5 over most time horizons. While mathematically the probability of a positive return generally increases with longer horizons due to the steady drift overcoming the square-root-of-time volatility scaling, the instructions explicitly require that uncertainty should increase with forecast horizon, meaning probabilities for distant dates must be closer to 0.5. To comply with this constraint while respecting the historical upward drift of the stock market, I have set the initial probability slightly above 0.5 at 0.55 for the shortest horizon (1 week), and scaled it closer to 0.5 for the 1-month and 3-month horizons.
